name: Hyrlo
description: 4 HP three-channel stereo audio mixer (3 stereo / up to 6 mono). L jack normals to R for mono use. Per-channel signal LED, AC-coupled, daisy-chainable for bigger buses. 34 mm deep, 40 mA per rail.

controls.name: Level knob CH 1
controls.description: Volume for channel 1. Optimised audio taper. Mutes fully at CCW. Stereo pair collapses to mono when only L is patched.
controls.detail: audio taper · 0 to unity · L normalled to R · signal LED next to knob

controls.name: Level knob CH 2
controls.description: Volume for channel 2. Same taper as CH 1. Independent stereo/mono behaviour per channel.
controls.detail: audio taper · 0 to unity · L normalled to R · signal LED next to knob

controls.name: Level knob CH 3
controls.description: Volume for channel 3. Same taper as CH 1. Bottom-most channel in daisy-chain order.
controls.detail: audio taper · 0 to unity · L normalled to R · signal LED next to knob

inputs.name: CH 1 L / R
inputs.description: Stereo pair input for channel 1. Patch only L for mono (R follows L). Patch both for true stereo source.

inputs.name: CH 2 L / R
inputs.description: Stereo pair input for channel 2. Normalling identical to CH 1.

inputs.name: CH 3 L / R
inputs.description: Stereo pair input for channel 3. Normalling identical to CH 1.

inputs.name: CHAIN IN L / R
inputs.description: Daisy-chain input. Sums an upstream Hyrlo (or other stereo source) into this module's output without using a CH 1-3 fader.

outputs.name: OUT L / R
outputs.description: Main stereo mix of CH 1-3 plus CHAIN IN. Transparent, low-noise summing.

behaviors.name: L → R mono normalling
behaviors.trigger: patch L jack only on a channel
behaviors.description: Each channel's L input normals to R. A mono cable in L sends the same signal to both sides of the stereo bus, keeping the channel centred. Patching R breaks the normal and opens true stereo.

behaviors.name: Daisy chain expansion
behaviors.trigger: patch one Hyrlo's OUT L/R into the next Hyrlo's CHAIN IN L/R
behaviors.description: Hyrlo's OUT can feed the next unit's CHAIN IN, building 6-, 9-, 12-channel stereo buses from stacked 4 HP modules. The downstream module's OUT carries the full sum.

behaviors.name: Flippable panel
behaviors.trigger: reverse panel orientation at install time
behaviors.description: Symmetrical faceplate lets the user mount the panel rotated 180°. Jacks and pots still align; cosmetic variant only. No signal-path changes.

behaviors.name: AC-coupled audio path
behaviors.description: All inputs and the output are AC-coupled, so DC offsets and slow CV are blocked. Use a dedicated CV mixer for control voltages.

behaviors.name: Per-channel signal LED
behaviors.description: A small LED next to each level knob brightens with the post-fader signal of that channel — quick visual check that a source is alive without unmuting the master.

patchIdeas.name: Three-voice stereo submix
patchIdeas.patch: Three stereo voices into CH 1-3 · main OUT to outboard · short signal path keeps transients intact.
steps: Set all three Level knobs fully CCW (muted) before patching.
Patch Voice 1 OUT L → Hyrlo CH 1 L, and Voice 1 OUT R → Hyrlo CH 1 R.
Repeat for Voice 2 into CH 2 L/R and Voice 3 into CH 3 L/R.
Patch Hyrlo OUT L → mixer/interface L, Hyrlo OUT R → mixer/interface R.
Bring up CH 1 to about noon, then CH 2, then CH 3, balancing by ear. The signal LEDs confirm each channel is alive.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / R — full stereo mix at line level, ready for an interface or recorder.
patchIdeas.hear: All three voices summed in true stereo. Pulling any cable from a channel's R input collapses that voice to centre; reinserting opens the stereo image again.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Hyrlo:
* CH 1-3 Level: start CCW, raise to taste

- Voice 1 (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 1 L)
- Voice 1 (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 1 R)
- Voice 2 (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 2 L)
- Voice 2 (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 2 R)
- Voice 3 (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 3 L)
- Voice 3 (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 3 R)
- Hyrlo (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Hyrlo (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Six-mono mix via two Hyrlos
patchIdeas.patch: Two stacked Hyrlos give six mono channels in 8 HP — drums + bass + pads in one skiff.
steps: Mount Hyrlo A (master) and Hyrlo B (sub) side by side. Levels all CCW.
Patch each mono drum to Hyrlo A's CH 1 L, CH 2 L, CH 3 L (R is left empty so each source is centred).
Patch bass / pad / lead to Hyrlo B's CH 1 L, CH 2 L, CH 3 L.
Patch Hyrlo B OUT L → Hyrlo A CHAIN IN L, and Hyrlo B OUT R → Hyrlo A CHAIN IN R.
Patch Hyrlo A OUT L/R to your interface. Bring up channels on B first (sub bus level is set by each B fader), then on A.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Hyrlo A OUT L / R — six mono sources summed to stereo.
patchIdeas.hear: Six independent mono voices in one centred stereo image. Hyrlo B has no master fader, so each B channel's knob is its own bus level into A.
patchIdeas.diagram:
HYRLO A:
* role: master

HYRLO B:
* role: sub-bus into A's CHAIN IN

- Kick (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 1 L)
- Snare (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 2 L)
- HiHat (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 3 L)
- Bass (Out) -> Hyrlo B (CH 1 L)
- Pad (Out) -> Hyrlo B (CH 2 L)
- Lead (Out) -> Hyrlo B (CH 3 L)
- Hyrlo B (OUT L) -> Hyrlo A (CHAIN IN L)
- Hyrlo B (OUT R) -> Hyrlo A (CHAIN IN R)
- Hyrlo A (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Hyrlo A (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Dry + two stereo FX returns
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 dry voice · CH 2 reverb return · CH 3 delay return · blend wet/dry on the front panel.
steps: Send the source voice to a 1-to-2 mult (or your sends) so one copy goes dry and the others feed the FX.
Patch the dry copy → Hyrlo CH 1 L (R empty, so it sits centre).
Patch the reverb's stereo return → Hyrlo CH 2 L and CH 2 R.
Patch the delay's stereo return → Hyrlo CH 3 L and CH 3 R.
Patch Hyrlo OUT L/R to the interface. CH 1 sets dry level; CH 2/CH 3 are independent wet faders.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / R — dry voice plus stereo reverb and delay returns mixed together.
patchIdeas.hear: A centred dry voice surrounded by stereo wet returns. Pulling CH 2 down kills the reverb without touching the delay, and vice versa — quick A/B of each effect.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Hyrlo:
* CH 1 = dry, CH 2 = reverb return, CH 3 = delay return

- Voice (Out) -> Mult (In)
- Mult (Out 1) -> Hyrlo (CH 1 L)
- Mult (Out 2) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 2 L)
- Reverb (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 2 R)
- Mult (Out 3) -> Delay (In)
- Delay (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 3 L)
- Delay (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 3 R)
- Hyrlo (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Hyrlo (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Drum sub-bus through outboard glue
patchIdeas.patch: Drum mix on Hyrlo A → external compressor → Hyrlo B CHAIN IN — bus-glue your kit before it hits the master.
steps: On Hyrlo A patch kick/snare/hat to CH 1 L / CH 2 L / CH 3 L. Levels start CCW.
Patch Hyrlo A OUT L → Compressor IN L, and Hyrlo A OUT R → Compressor IN R.
Patch Compressor OUT L/R → Hyrlo B CHAIN IN L/R. Hyrlo B's CH 1-3 are still free for bass / synths.
Patch Hyrlo B OUT L/R to your interface.
Drive the compressor with Hyrlo A's three faders; set Hyrlo B's chain bus by adjusting the compressor's makeup gain (CHAIN IN has no fader).
patchIdeas.signalOut: Hyrlo B OUT L / R — bus-compressed drum sub plus uncompressed CH 1-3 sources.
patchIdeas.hear: Drums sound glued and a touch tighter while bass/synth on Hyrlo B stay clean. Bypass the compressor to A/B; raise A's individual faders to push more drums into the compressor harder.
patchIdeas.diagram:
HYRLO A:
* role: drum sub

HYRLO B:
* role: master, CHAIN IN fed by compressor

- Kick (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 1 L)
- Snare (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 2 L)
- HiHat (Out) -> Hyrlo A (CH 3 L)
- Hyrlo A (OUT L) -> Compressor (In L)
- Hyrlo A (OUT R) -> Compressor (In R)
- Compressor (Out L) -> Hyrlo B (CHAIN IN L)
- Compressor (Out R) -> Hyrlo B (CHAIN IN R)
- Hyrlo B (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Hyrlo B (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo synth + two mono drums in 4 HP
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 stereo synth (L/R both patched) · CH 2/3 mono drum sources (L only, auto-centred) — entire jam in one 4 HP module.
steps: Patch your stereo synth's OUT L → Hyrlo CH 1 L and OUT R → Hyrlo CH 1 R.
Patch mono Drum 1 → Hyrlo CH 2 L (leave CH 2 R empty so it centres).
Patch mono Drum 2 → Hyrlo CH 3 L (leave CH 3 R empty).
Patch Hyrlo OUT L/R to your interface.
Bring up CH 1 first to set the stereo bed, then CH 2/CH 3 to taste. The signal LEDs flash with each drum hit.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / R — wide stereo synth with two centred mono drum voices on top.
patchIdeas.hear: A wide stereo pad with the drums punching through the centre. If you later patch a cable into CH 2 R, the snare jumps off-centre — useful for hard-pan tricks.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Hyrlo:
* CH 1 = stereo, CH 2/3 = mono (centred)

- Stereo Synth (Out L) -> Hyrlo (CH 1 L)
- Stereo Synth (Out R) -> Hyrlo (CH 1 R)
- Drum 1 (Out) -> Hyrlo (CH 2 L)
- Drum 2 (Out) -> Hyrlo (CH 3 L)
- Hyrlo (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Hyrlo (OUT R) -> Output (R)
